methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $703M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Improbable Defence, a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Herman Narula, has raised $703M to date. The broader Defense & Security sector includes 10 identified peers (2 public, 8 private), among them Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Shield AI, though direct same-stage comparables remain sparse.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as the peer-median approach lacks sufficient narrow-stage datapoints (fewer than 3 comparable Growth-stage companies in the Defense & Security space with aligned valuation tiers). The stage-progression method applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $703M capital raised proxy, implying a valuation of $844M. This reflects typical capital efficiency expectations for Growth-stage advancement.

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $717M to $970M, centered at $844M. The spread between low and high estimates—approximately $253M or 35% of the median—reflects material uncertainty around capital efficiency assumptions and the lack of sector-specific comparable transactions at this stage. Key unknowns include revenue run-rate, unit economics, and contract concentration, which would refine both the stage multiplier and peer-relative positioning. Any revised valuation should incorporate evidence of these operating metrics rather than reliance on capital raised alone.
